examples.title: Packet Encapsulation Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how data is encapsulated as it moves down the OSI layers
examples.code:
def encapsulate_data(application_data):
    # Start with application data
    data = application_data
    
    # Presentation Layer - Data formatting and encryption
    presentation_header = "PRES"  # Represents formatting/encryption
    data = presentation_header + data
    print(f"Presentation Layer: Added header, length = {len(data)}")
    
    # Session Layer - Session management
    session_header = "SESS"  # Session ID
    data = session_header + data
    print(f"Session Layer: Added header, length = {len(data)}")
    
    # Transport Layer - Segmentation with TCP/UDP
    transport_header = "TCP|src_port:80|dst_port:443|"
    segment = transport_header + data
    print(f"Transport Layer: Segment created, length = {len(segment)}")
    
    # Network Layer - Packet with IP addressing
    network_header = "IP|src:192.168.1.10|dst:10.0.0.1|"
    packet = network_header + segment
    print(f"Network Layer: Packet created, length = {len(packet)}")
    
    # Data Link Layer - Framing with MAC addresses
    frame_header = "MAC|src:AA:BB:CC:DD|dest:11:22:33:44|"
    frame_trailer = "CRC:0x1A2B"
    frame = frame_header + packet + frame_trailer
    print(f"Data Link Layer: Frame created, length = {len(frame)}")
    
    return frame

# Simulate sending data
original_message = "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1"
print(f"Original Application Data: {original_message}\n")
final_frame = encapsulate_data(original_message)
print(f"\nFinal Frame transmitted: {final_frame}")
print("\nPhysical Layer: Frame converted to bits and transmitted on network medium")
examples.explanation: This code simulates the encapsulation process showing how each OSI layer adds its header (and trailer at Data Link) to the original application data. Each layer transforms the data into its Protocol Data Unit (PDU).
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) - storing encapsulated data at each step

examples.title: Network Packet Forwarding by Router
examples.description: Demonstrates how a router operates at Layer 3 (Network Layer) of the OSI model
examples.code:
CLASS RouterOperation
BEGIN
    FUNCTION receive_frame(frame)
    BEGIN
        // Physical Layer receives bits and converts to frame
        PRINT "Physical Layer: Received bits, converted to frame"
        
        // Data Link Layer: Check destination MAC
        IF frame.dest_mac == router_interface_mac THEN
            PRINT "Data Link Layer: Frame addressed to router"
            remove frame_header_and_trailer
            packet = frame.payload
        ELSE
            PRINT "Data Link Layer: Frame discarded - not for this router"
            RETURN
        ENDIF
        
        // Network Layer: Process IP packet
        PRINT "Network Layer: Processing IP packet"
        
        // Extract destination IP from packet header
        dest_ip = packet.header.destination_ip
        PRINT "Destination IP: " + dest_ip
        
        // Routing decision - lookup routing table
        next_hop = routing_table_lookup(dest_ip)
        
        IF next_hop != NULL THEN
            PRINT "Network Layer: Route found, next hop: " + next_hop
            
            // Decrement TTL
            packet.header.ttl = packet.header.ttl - 1
            
            IF packet.header.ttl > 0 THEN
                // Re-encapsulate for next segment
                new_frame = encapsulate_for_next_hop(packet, next_hop)
                transmit(new_frame)
            ELSE
                PRINT "Network Layer: Packet dropped - TTL expired"
            ENDIF
        ELSE
            PRINT "Network Layer: No route to destination - packet dropped"
        ENDIF
    END
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how a router operates at the Network Layer (Layer 3). It receives frames at Layer 2, strips the Data Link information, processes the IP packet, makes routing decisions based on the routing table, and forwards the packet toward the destination.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log n) for routing table lookup using efficient structures like tries or hash tables

examples.title: TCP Segment Structure
examples.description: Demonstrates the Transport Layer PDU structure with source and destination ports
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates the structure of headers at different OSI layers. The Transport Layer (TCP) adds a segment header with port numbers, sequence numbers, and flags. The Network Layer (IP) adds its header with source and destination IP addresses. The Data Link Layer adds MAC addresses. Each layer encapsulates the layer above's data.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - constant time operations for header manipulation
